name: Streamarr
description: Monitor Twitch, YouTube, Kick and other streamlink-supported platforms for live content (and Twitch VODs); auto-create and remove channels. Per-platform settings groups, with auto-migration of legacy monitored_channels.

settings.label: Core Setup
settings.description: Required wiring: streamlink profile and target playlists.

fields.label: Stream Profile (streamlink)
fields.helper_text:
Select a Stream Profile with the 'Streamlink' backend. FFmpeg profiles will NOT work for Twitch streams.

⚠ You must first create a Stream Profile under Settings → Stream Profiles with backend set to 'Streamlink' and quality 'best'. Cookies configured on this profile will also be used for stream detection.

fields.label: Target Playlist (Standard)
fields.helper_text: Standard playlist to associate created channels with. Leave empty to skip.

fields.label: Target Playlist (Custom)
fields.helper_text: Custom playlist to add created channels to. Leave empty to skip.

settings.label: Stream & Live Defaults
settings.description: Defaults applied to every platform unless that platform overrides them.

fields.label: Live Channel Group
fields.helper_text: Group name for live-stream channels. Used in static mode, and as fallback in game mode when the game is unknown.

fields.label: Group Mode
fields.helper_text: Static uses the fixed group name below. Game mode groups live channels by their current Twitch game/category (e.g. 'Just Chatting', 'Fortnite'). Game mode requires Twitch API credentials and falls back to static without them. VOD channels always use the VOD Group name.

fields.label: EPG Mode

options.game: Game / Category (shows game changes as programme entries)
options.title: Stream Title (single programme with the stream title)

fields.helper_text: Game mode creates a new programme entry each time the streamer switches games. Title mode shows a single continuous programme using the stream title. useful if you prefer all channels under one group with simpler EPG.

fields.label: Live Channel Title Mode
fields.helper_text: Controls the title of live channels. Stream Title uses the current Twitch stream title. Channel Name uses the Twitch channel display name.

fields.label: Stream Quality
fields.helper_text: Preferred stream quality. Streamlink will select the closest available quality.

fields.label: Output Format
fields.helper_text: Container format for created channels. Must match the format configured on the linked Stream Profile. Changing this setting updates all existing channels on the next Check Now.

fields.label: Auto-cleanup Ended Streams
fields.helper_text: Automatically delete live-stream channels when the stream ends. Runs during each monitoring cycle. VOD channels are never auto-cleaned.

fields.label: Twitch Channels
fields.helper_text:
One Twitch login per line. Decimal numbering supported via username=BaseNumber.
  nightbot
  pokimane=50
Lines starting with # are ignored.

Without Twitch API credentials each channel is checked individually via streamlink, which is slow for more than ~20 channels.

fields.label: Twitch Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for Twitch live channels. Leave empty to use the global Channel Group from Stream Defaults.

fields.label: Include Twitch VODs
fields.helper_text: Also create channels for recent VODs of monitored Twitch channels. Requires Twitch API credentials below. VOD discovery is not available via streamlink.

fields.label: Twitch VODs per Channel
fields.helper_text: Max recent VODs per Twitch channel (1-100).

fields.label: Twitch VOD Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for Twitch VOD channels. Leave empty to use the global Channel Group from Stream Defaults.

fields.label: Twitch Client ID
fields.helper_text: Helix App Access Token Client ID. Create an application at https://dev.twitch.tv/console/apps with OAuth Redirect URL set to http://localhost and Category set to 'Application Integration'.

fields.label: Twitch Client Secret
fields.helper_text: Helix Client Secret from the same Twitch application. Stored encrypted.

settings.label: YouTube
settings.description: YouTube live streams. Detection uses streamlink by default; supplying an optional YouTube Data API v3 key switches to the official API for faster, rate-limit-free probing. VOD support is planned for a later release.

fields.label: YouTube Channels
fields.helper_text:
One YouTube channel per line. Accepted formats:
  @ChannelHandle
  https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelHandle
  https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xxxx
Lines starting with # are ignored.

Detection uses streamlink and currently supports live streams only. Cookies from the linked Stream Profile are passed automatically.

fields.label: YouTube Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for YouTube live channels. Leave empty to use the global Channel Group from Stream Defaults.

fields.label: YouTube Data API v3 Key (optional)
fields.helper_text: Optional. Provide a YouTube Data API v3 key to use the official API for live detection instead of streamlink. Faster and avoids HTTP 429 rate-limits when monitoring many channels. Cost: ~100 quota units per channel check; the default 10000 daily quota covers about 100 channel checks per day. Without a key, streamlink is used (unchanged behavior). Get a key at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/youtube.googleapis.com

fields.label: Kick Channels
fields.helper_text:
One Kick channel per line. Accepted formats:
  https://kick.com/trainwreckstv
  kick:trainwreckstv
Lines starting with # are ignored.

Note: a bare slug without the 'kick:' prefix would conflict with Twitch bare logins, so it is not accepted as a Kick channel.

Live detection uses the public Kick API (no key required); streamlink is used as a fallback when the API is unreachable. Find live channels at https://kick.com/browse/categories

fields.label: Kick Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for Kick live channels. Leave empty to use the global Channel Group from Stream Defaults.

fields.label: Include Kick VODs
fields.helper_text: When on, recent past broadcasts (VODs) for the Kick channels above are added as separate entries.

fields.label: Max Kick VODs per Channel
fields.helper_text: Upper bound on how many recent VODs to import per Kick channel (1-50).

fields.label: Kick VOD Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for Kick VOD entries. Leave empty to derive from the Kick live group with a 'VODs' suffix.

fields.label: Force Streamlink for Kick
fields.helper_text: When on, the Kick API is bypassed and streamlink is used directly. Useful if the Kick API is consistently blocked by Cloudflare in your environment.

settings.label: Generic / Other Platforms
settings.description: Any other platform that streamlink supports.

fields.label: Generic Channels
fields.helper_text:
One URL per line for any platform that streamlink supports. Examples:
  https://www.vimeo.com/event/<id>
  https://rumble.com/<video-id>
  https://www.bilibili.com/<id>
  https://dlive.tv/<channel>
  https://afreecatv.com/<channel>
  https://trovo.live/s/<channel>
  https://www.nicovideo.jp/watch/<id>
  https://ok.ru/live/<id>
  https://www.huya.com/<id>
  https://www.douyu.com/<id>
Lines starting with # are ignored.

Only live streams. VOD discovery is not supported for generic platforms.
Full list of supported platforms: https://streamlink.github.io/plugins.html

fields.label: Generic Group Label
fields.helper_text: Optional. M3U group-title for generic platform live channels. Leave empty to use the global Channel Group from Stream Defaults.

settings.label: Numbering & Xtream
settings.description: Channel numbering and Xtream API compatibility.

fields.label: Starting Channel Number
fields.helper_text: First channel number to assign. Each new stream increments from here. For best Xtream client compatibility, keep this in a low range (for example 900-999).

fields.label: Channel Number Increment
fields.helper_text: How much to increment the channel number for each new stream.

fields.label: Channel Numbering Mode
fields.helper_text: Decimal mode groups streams from the same Twitch channel using the channel's BaseNumber. Sequential mode uses a global counter.

fields.label: Xtream Compatibility Mode
fields.helper_text: When enabled, Streamarr keeps channel numbers in a bounded range to avoid clients that hide high channel numbers.

fields.label: Xtream Min Channel Number
fields.helper_text: Lower bound for Xtream compatibility numbering.

fields.label: Xtream Max Channel Number
fields.helper_text: Upper bound for Xtream compatibility numbering. Existing plugin channels outside this range are auto-renumbered on Check Now.

fields.label: Xtream ASCII Text Compatibility
fields.helper_text: Removes emoji and problematic special characters from Streamarr channel names/titles to improve compatibility with strict Xtream clients.

fields.label: Enable Automatic Monitoring
fields.helper_text: Run monitoring automatically on the schedule below. Requires the scheduler to be running (php artisan schedule:run).

fields.label: Monitor Schedule (cron)
fields.helper_text: Cron expression for automatic monitoring. Default: every 10 minutes. Examples: */5 * * * * (every 5 min), 0 * * * * (hourly).

fields.label: Twitch URL(s)
fields.helper_text: One or more Twitch channel or VOD URLs (newline or comma-separated). Examples: https://twitch.tv/pokimane, https://twitch.tv/videos/123456789

fields.label: Stream URL
fields.helper_text: Any URL supported by streamlink (e.g. https://twitch.tv/pokimane or https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelHandle). Returns live status, metadata, and available stream qualities. Cookies from the configured Stream Profile are automatically used.
